selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw:
{
"mode": "llm",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "Creating a robust and reliable AI-powered system for detecting and mitigating the spread of misinformation on social media is an essential step towards promoting a more informed public discourse. By leveraging natural language processing and machine learning techniques, such a system could automatically identify and flag fake content in real-time, allowing users to quickly and easily verify the accuracy of information shared on social media platforms.",
"reasoning_summary": "To build an effective AI-powered misinformation detection system, we must first develop sophisticated algorithms that can accurately identify and classify different types of fake content, such as disinformation, propaganda, and clickbait. By analyzing large datasets of labeled content, these algorithms can learn to recognize patterns and features associated with each type of misinformation, enabling them to make accurate predictions in new, unseen contexts.",
"tone": "authoritative",
"citations": [],
"safety_notes": "",
"used_persona_fields": []
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: Creating a robust and reliable AI-powered system for detecting and mitigating the spread of misinformation on social media is an essential step towards promoting a more informed public discourse. By leveraging natural language processing and machine learning techniques, such a system could automatically identify and flag fake content in real-time, allowing users to quickly and easily verify the accuracy of information shared on social media platforms.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To build an effective AI-powered misinformation detection system, we must first develop sophisticated algorithms that can accurately identify and classify different types of fake content, such as disinformation, propaganda, and clickbait. By analyzing large datasets of labeled content, these algorithms can learn to recognize patterns and features associated with each type of misinformation, enabling them to make accurate predictions in new, unseen contexts.
